hook_type: Identity statement, second-person, FMTH belonging-equivalent ('FarmThru families know who grew their food')
tactic: Vote-with-wallet bridge -> brand grid social proof -> recursion punch
primary_text:
You vote with your wallet every time you buy from an Aussie brand. We let you vote with ownership.

Since 2018, Birchal has helped 324 Australian companies raise over $234M from 137,000 retail investors. Naked Life. Our Cow. Medigrowth. People who used to be customers became part-owners.

Now we're opening our own raise.

Save your spot. Read the offer when it opens.

*Always consider the general CSF risk warning and offer document before investing.
headline: Australians who back Aussie brands
description: You buy from them already. Now you can own a piece.
_v2_changes: Subject discipline: 'we' = Birchal-the-company throughout. One 'Birchal' mention in para 2 to anchor brand identity. Old V1 'Birchal lets you' / 'Birchal is now doing the same for itself' confusion eliminated.
Trimmed 'None of those raises came from VCs alone. They came from 137,000 everyday Australians' (rephrasing) to 'People who used to be customers became part-owners' (one line, identity-led).
Dropped redundant 'who wanted to own a piece of what they had been buying' (clause repeats the lead).
_drc_playbook: 03 Aspirational Identity Sell (B2C considered, solution-aware, sophistication 3 mechanism)
_drc_audience: B2C considered, solution-aware. First-time CSF investors who self-identify as backing Australian-made / Aussie-owned.
_compliance_findings_resolved: PREOFFER-VERBS: 'save your spot', 'read', 'own a piece'. No transaction verbs.
VERBATIM-OFFER-LANGUAGE: 'Read the offer when it opens'.
BRAND-EXCLUDED: only verified-good raises (Naked Life, Our Cow, Medigrowth).
